# Test Reports Guide

Comprehensive guide to generating, viewing, and understanding test reports for the e-commerce platform.

## 🎯 Quick Start

### Generate Full Test Report

```bash
# Generate comprehensive HTML report
make test-tenant-report

# View the report
make view-tenant-report
```

### Generate Specific Reports

```bash
# Coverage report only
make test-tenant-coverage
make view-tenant-coverage

# Test badges
make test-tenant-badges

# CI-style run with JSON output
make test-tenant-ci
```

## 📊 Report Types

### 1. Interactive HTML Report

**Generated by**: `make test-tenant-report`

**Features**:
- 📈 Real-time test statistics
- 📊 Interactive coverage visualization
- 📋 Test results by package
- 🎨 Color-coded pass/fail/skip indicators
- ⏱️ Performance metrics (duration per test)
- 📦 Coverage breakdown by package

**Location**: `services/tenant-service/test-reports/latest/index.html`

**How to View**:
```bash
# Option 1: Using make command
make view-tenant-report

# Option 2: Direct open
open services/tenant-service/test-reports/latest/index.html

# Option 3: Local server
cd services/tenant-service/test-reports/latest
python3 -m http.server 8000
# Then visit http://localhost:8000
```

### 2. Coverage HTML Report

**Generated by**: `make test-tenant-coverage`

**Features**:
- Line-by-line coverage visualization
- Green (covered) / Red (not covered) highlighting
- Function-level coverage stats
- File browser

**Location**: `services/tenant-service/coverage.html`

### 3. Coverage Summary (Text)

**Location**: `services/tenant-service/test-reports/latest/coverage-summary.txt`

**Example Output**:
```
github.com/ecommerce/tenant-service/internal/api/tenant_handler.go:15:      CreateTenant       100.0%
github.com/ecommerce/tenant-service/internal/api/tenant_handler.go:30:      GetTenant          100.0%
github.com/ecommerce/tenant-service/internal/repository/tenant_repository.go:20:    NewTenantRepository    100.0%
github.com/ecommerce/tenant-service/internal/repository/tenant_repository.go:26:    Create                 87.5%
github.com/ecommerce/tenant-service/internal/service/tenant_service.go:35:  NewTenantService   100.0%
github.com/ecommerce/tenant-service/internal/service/tenant_service.go:42:  CreateTenant       92.3%
total:  (statements)   82.5%
```

### 4. JSON Test Results

**Location**: `services/tenant-service/test-reports/latest/tests.json`

**Format**: Newline-delimited JSON (each line is a JSON event)

**Example**:
```json
{"Time":"2024-04-16T10:30:00Z","Action":"run","Package":"github.com/ecommerce/tenant-service/internal/repository","Test":"TestTenantRepositoryTestSuite"}
{"Time":"2024-04-16T10:30:00.015Z","Action":"pass","Package":"github.com/ecommerce/tenant-service/internal/repository","Test":"TestTenantRepositoryTestSuite/TestCreate","Elapsed":0.01}
```

**Use Cases**:
- CI/CD integration
- Custom report generation
- Test result analysis tools
- Historical tracking

### 5. Test Badges

**Generated by**: `make test-tenant-badges`

**Location**: `services/tenant-service/badges/`

**Files Generated**:
- `coverage.svg` - Coverage percentage badge
- `coverage.json` - Shields.io endpoint JSON
- `tests.svg` - Total tests badge
- `tests.json` - Shields.io endpoint JSON
- `passing.json` - Build status badge

**Usage in README**:
```markdown
![Coverage](./badges/coverage.svg)
![Tests](./badges/tests.svg)
![Build](https://github.com/YOUR_ORG/YOUR_REPO/actions/workflows/tenant-service-tests.yml/badge.svg)
```

## 📁 Report Structure

```
services/tenant-service/
├── test-reports/
│   ├── test-report-20240416_103000/    # Timestamped report
│   │   ├── index.html                  # Main interactive report
│   │   ├── coverage.html               # Detailed coverage
│   │   ├── coverage.out                # Go coverage data
│   │   ├── coverage-summary.txt        # Text summary
│   │   ├── tests.json                  # Raw test results
│   │   ├── test-output.log             # Console output
│   │   └── REPORT.md                   # Markdown summary
│   └── latest -> test-report-20240416_103000/  # Symlink to latest
├── badges/
│   ├── coverage.svg
│   ├── coverage.json
│   ├── tests.svg
│   ├── tests.json
│   └── README_BADGES.md
├── coverage.html                       # Quick coverage view
└── coverage.out                        # Coverage data
```

## 🔄 CI/CD Integration

### GitHub Actions

The repository includes a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically:

1. ✅ Runs all tests on push/PR
2. ✅ Generates coverage reports
3. ✅ Checks 80% coverage threshold
4. ✅ Comments on PRs with results
5. ✅ Uploads reports as artifacts
6. ✅ Sends coverage to Codecov

**Workflow File**: `.github/workflows/tenant-service-tests.yml`

**View in GitHub**:
- Go to Actions tab
- Click on latest workflow run
- Download artifacts (test-results, coverage-reports)

### Manual CI-style Run

```bash
# Run tests like CI does
make test-tenant-ci

# This generates:
# - test-results.json (JSON output)
# - coverage.out (coverage data)
# - Runs with race detection
```

## 📈 Understanding Test Results

### Test Status Indicators

| Status | Color | Meaning |
|--------|-------|---------|
| ✅ PASS | Green | Test passed successfully |
| ❌ FAIL | Red | Test failed |
| ⚠️ SKIP | Yellow | Test was skipped |

### Coverage Metrics

| Coverage | Color | Status |
|----------|-------|--------|
| 80%+ | Green | Excellent ✅ |
| 60-79% | Yellow | Good ⚠️ |
| <60% | Red | Needs improvement ❌ |

### Performance Metrics

Each test shows duration:
- `< 0.01s` - Excellent ⚡
- `0.01s - 0.1s` - Good ✅
- `0.1s - 1s` - Acceptable ⚠️
- `> 1s` - Slow 🐌 (consider optimization)

## 🎨 Report Features

### Interactive HTML Report

1. **Summary Dashboard**
   - Total tests, passed, failed, skipped
   - Overall coverage percentage
   - Total test duration
   - Visual coverage bar

2. **Test Results by Package**
   - Grouped by Go package
   - Individual test status
   - Test duration
   - Searchable and filterable

3. **Coverage by Package**
   - Coverage percentage per package
   - Visual progress bars
   - Sortable by coverage

4. **Quick Links**
   - Jump to detailed coverage
   - View coverage summary
   - Download raw JSON data

### Coverage HTML Report

- **Source Code View**: See actual code with highlighting
- **Line Numbers**: Exact coverage per line
- **Function Coverage**: Which functions are tested
- **File Browser**: Navigate through codebase
- **Coverage Stats**: Top of page shows file stats

### Sample Test Summary

```
========================================
Test Summary
========================================
Total Tests:    35
Passed:         35
Failed:         0
Skipped:        0
Coverage:       82.5%
Duration:       2.45s
Status:         ✅ PASSING
========================================
```

### Sample Coverage by Package

```
Package                                    Coverage
-------------------------------------------------
internal/repository                        85.2%
internal/service                           82.1%
internal/api                               79.8%
internal/middleware                        78.5%
pkg/database                               65.0%
pkg/kafka                                  60.0%
pkg/logger                                 55.0%
-------------------------------------------------
Total                                      82.5%
```

## 🚀 Advanced Usage

### Generate Report for Specific Package

```bash
cd services/tenant-service
go test -v -coverprofile=coverage.out ./internal/repository/...
go tool cover -html=coverage.out -o repository-coverage.html
```

### Compare Coverage Over Time

```bash
# Generate baseline
make test-tenant-coverage
cp services/tenant-service/coverage.out baseline-coverage.out

# Make changes, then compare
make test-tenant-coverage
go tool covdata diff -i baseline-coverage.out -j coverage.out
```

### Generate Report in Different Formats

```bash
cd services/tenant-service

# JSON report with gotestsum
gotestsum --format standard-verbose --jsonfile report.json

# JUnit XML (for Jenkins, CircleCI, etc.)
gotestsum --junitfile report.xml

# Short summary
gotestsum --format short

# With coverage
gotestsum --format testname -- -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
```

### Custom Report Styling

Edit `scripts/generate_test_report.sh` and modify the CSS in the HTML template section:

```css
/* Example: Change primary color */
.header {
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #your-color 0%, #your-color-2 100%);
}
```

## 🔍 Troubleshooting

### Report Not Generated

```bash
# Check if gotestsum is installed
which gotestsum

# Install if missing
go install gotest.tools/gotestsum@latest

# Re-run report generation
make test-tenant-report
```

### Coverage Shows 0%

```bash
# Make sure you're running tests with coverage
go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...

# Check if coverage.out exists
ls -la coverage.out

# Regenerate report
make test-tenant-report
```

### Can't Open HTML Report

```bash
# On macOS
open services/tenant-service/test-reports/latest/index.html

# On Linux
xdg-open services/tenant-service/test-reports/latest/index.html

# Or use a local server
cd services/tenant-service/test-reports/latest
python3 -m http.server 8000
# Visit http://localhost:8000
```

- [Go Testing Package](https://pkg.go.dev/testing)
- [Go Coverage Tool](https://go.dev/blog/cover)

### Tools Used
- [gotestsum](https://github.com/gotestyourself/gotestsum) - Test runner with formatted output
- [testify](https://github.com/stretchr/testify) - Testing assertions and mocks

### CI/CD
- [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions)
- [Codecov](https://codecov.io/)
- [SonarQube](https://www.sonarqube.org/)

## 🎯 Best Practices

1. **Run tests before committing**
   ```bash
   make test-tenant
   ```

2. **Check coverage regularly**
   ```bash
   make test-tenant-coverage
   ```

3. **Generate full report for releases**
   ```bash
   make test-tenant-report
   ```

4. **Keep coverage above 80%**
   - Check with `make test-tenant-coverage`
   - CI will fail if below 80%

5. **Review failed tests immediately**
   - Check report for details
   - Fix before pushing

6. **Update badges after changes**
   ```bash
   make test-tenant-badges
   ```

## 📞 Support

If you encounter issues:

1. Check this guide
2. Review test output logs
3. Check GitHub Actions logs
4. Contact the development team

---

**Happy Testing!** 🧪✨
